owl:Class.rdfs:label: Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable or observable characteristic of an organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Transparency
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism characterized by the presence of transparent tissues.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Size
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A morphological characteristic, easy to measure, conspicuous, ecologically important, related to the overall physical magnitude of an organism, comparable across taxa and variable through time and space

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The distance from point to point along the longest axis of the body of an organism
mm µm (microzooplankton)

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Width
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The distance from side to side of the body of an organism, perpendicular to the axis along which lenght is measured
mm µm (microzooplankton)

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Mass
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable characteristic related to the body mass of an organism.
mg

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Wet Mass
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of a whole fresh organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Dry Mass
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of an organism after removal of body fluids drying at 50-70 °C (typically at 60 °C) in a drying oven for 24 hours (or until constant weight, particularly for gelatinous organisms).
mg

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Zooplankton Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable or observable characteristic related to zooplankton species

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ash Free Dry Mass
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The mass of organic material of an organism obtained as difference between the dry mass and the ash weigth.
mg

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ash Content
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of the residual after combusting the organism at typically 500°C. Ash weight is often expressed as a percentage of dry mass

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Biovolume
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Total volume of a single organism. It is calculated according to the geometric equations, after association of geometric shape to the real shape of the organism and measurements of linear dimensions (length, width). It is mainly used for microzooplankton. It is used for mesozooplankton when samples are analysed with image systems.
μm3

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Carbon Content
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Carbon content of zooplankton organism determined for microzooplankton indirectly by converting the individual organism biovolume into carbon using empirically or theoretically derived equations and for meso and macrozooplankton by direct analysis of individual organisms or bulk community at the CHN elemental analyser by using fresh, fresh frozen and fresh dried samples.

µgCL-1 (microzooplankton)
µgC (mesozooplankton)

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Shape
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The approximate shape of an organism, described by considering the cross-section of organisms body

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ellipsoid
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates an ellipsoid

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Parabolic segment
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a parabolic segment

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated cone + parabolic segment with equal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a truncated cone + parabolic segment with equal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated cone + hemisphere
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a truncated cone + hemisphere

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Segment of ellipsoid
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a segment of ellipsoid

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Functional Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any morphological, behavioural, physiological or phenological trait, which impacts zooplankton fitness

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated Cone + Truncated Cone
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates two truncated cones

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cone + truncated cone with equal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cone + truncated cone with equal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Elliptic parabolic segment + elliptic cone
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates an elliptic parabolic segment + elliptic cone

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Parabolic segment + ellipsoid
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a parabolic segment + ellipsoid

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cone + Half Sphere
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cone + half sphere

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Parabolic segment + hemisphere
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a parabolic segment + hemisphere

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cylinder + Half Sphere
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cylinder + half sphere

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cylinder + ellipsoid with equal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cylinder + ellipsoid with equal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cylinder + parabolic segment with equal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cylinder + parabolic segment with equal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cone + truncated cone with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cone + truncated cone with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Morphological Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable phenotypic characteristic (form and structure) of an organism, which is relatively simple to measure, easily observable and with a potentially well-defined relation to physiology and ecology.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cylinder + ellipsoid with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cylinder + ellipsoid with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cylinder + parabolic segment with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cylinder + parabolic segment with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated cone + ellipsoid with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a truncated cone + ellipsoid with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated cone + parabolic segment with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a truncated cone + parabolic segment with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Elliptic parabolic segment + elliptic ellipsoid with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates an elliptic parabolic segment + elliptic ellipsoid with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Elliptic cone + elliptic ellipsoid with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates an elliptic cone + elliptic ellipsoid with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Elliptic cone + elliptic parabolic segment with unequal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates an elliptic cone + elliptic parabolic segment with unequal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated cone + ellipsoid with equal heights
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a truncated cone + ellipsoid with equal heights

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Sphere
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a sphere

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cone
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximates a cone

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Presence of Cilia
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism characterized by the presence of cilia.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Presence of Flagella
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism characterized by the presence of flagella.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Presence of Lorica
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism characterized by the presence of a lorica.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Jelly factor
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Gelatinous plankton characterised by a high water content, or "jelly factor"

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cylinder
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximate a cylinder

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pyramid
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximate a pyramid

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Spheroid
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximate a spheroid

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated Pyramid
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximate a truncated pyramid

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Truncated cone
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A body shape that approximate a truncated cone

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Food Particle Size Range
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The size spectra of food particles (feeding kernels) for different size zooplankton. The frequency distribution of food particles ingested, could help describe and compare diets and the effects of different groups of zooplankton on phytoplankton.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Presence of Calcitic Shell
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism characterized by the presence of a calcitic shell.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Behavioural Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any behavioural characteristic evolved by species allowing them to exploit the given combination of resources represented in their niche.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Presence of Siliceous Exoskeletal Structures
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism characterized by the presence of siliceous exoskeletal structures.

cq:CompetencyQuestion.skos:definition: What is the superclass for 'Coloniality'?
What is the classification of 'Presence of Calcitic Shell'?
What is the definition of the body shape 'Truncated cone + hemisphere'?
How is 'Body Mass' defined?
Is 'Food Particle Size Range' a type of 'Morphological Trait'?
Which traits related to 'Body Mass' have defined synonyms?
Is 'Ash Free Dry Mass' a direct subclass of 'Body Size'?
What is the definition of 'Body Length'?
List all direct subtypes of 'Morphological Trait'.
Which is the most general class in the ontology?
What is the measurement unit for 'Biovolume'?
For all subclasses of 'Body Shape' that involve a 'Cylinder', provide their definitions.
How does the 'Food Particle Size Range' trait help in ecological studies?
What are all the specific geometric shapes that are subtypes of 'Body Shape'?
What is the parent class for the 'Spheroid' body shape?
What are the characteristics of a 'Functional Trait'?
Does the ontology define a trait for 'Coloniality'?
List all direct subtypes of 'Morphological Trait'.
Is 'Body Size' classified as a 'Functional Trait'?
What types of 'Body Size' measurements exist and what are their units?
What is the described procedure for determining 'Dry Mass'?
What is the hierarchical path from 'Ash Content' to 'Trait'?
What is the parent class for the 'Spheroid' body shape?
List all 'Morphological Traits' that are not related to organism size or shape.
In what units is 'Body Width' typically measured?
What is the definition of a 'Parabolic segment + hemisphere' body shape?
Is 'Zooplankton Trait' a subclass of 'Trait'?
What does 'Ash Content' mean?
Which traits related to 'Body Mass' have defined synonyms?
Which types of zooplankton is 'Biovolume' primarily measured for?
Which traits are defined by the 'presence of' a specific structure?
What are the characteristics of a 'Functional Trait'?
What is the typical combustion temperature for measuring 'Ash Content'?
What is the definition of a 'Parabolic segment + hemisphere' body shape?
Is 'Ash Free Dry Mass' a direct subclass of 'Body Size'?
Is 'Dry Mass' a type of 'Body Size'?
What is the definition of the trait that has 'Dry Organic Mass' as a synonym?
List all 'Body Shape' subtypes that are described as a combination of two geometric forms.
What are all the specific geometric shapes that are subtypes of 'Body Shape'?
What is the superclass of 'Food Particle Size Range'?
What are all the specific geometric shapes that are subtypes of 'Body Shape'?

What methods are used to determine 'Carbon Content'?
